─[ Proxmox Bridge Rename ]─ ┐

name: proxmox-bridge-rename description: Renaming Proxmox bridges and updating VM/CT attachments user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob

Proxmox Bridge Rename

Context

Safely renaming Proxmox bridge interfaces (e.g., vmbr-mgmt -> vmbr2) across cluster nodes to standardize naming or correct VLAN role descriptions.

Snags

  • In-use Ports: ifreload -a may fail if the bridge has active ports that cannot be moved or if the kernel has the device locked.
  • Cluster Outages: Applying network changes cluster-wide can cause a full outage if a mistake is made in the bridge configuration.
  • VM/CT Disconnects: VMs and containers won’t have network access until their configuration is updated to the new bridge name.

Workaround

  1. Inventory & Backup: Record current bridge usage and backup /etc/network/interfaces on all nodes.
  2. Update Configs: Update bridge names in /etc/network/interfaces on each node.
  3. Apply Changes: One node at a time. If ifreload -a fails, reboot the node.
  4. Update VM/CTs: Update bridge attachments in Proxmox VM/CT configuration files.
  5. Verify: Check ip link show <bridge> and ha-manager status (if HA is used).

Safety Rules

  • Use a maintenance window and console access.
  • Apply changes one node at a time.
  • Standardize names for IaC consistency.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
